preview_text:
The model favours Australia to win, assigning the side a 54% chance of victory on a neutral ground. Egypt are given a 17% probability, leaving Australia a substantial edge in the forecast.

The expected distribution suggests a competitive match with multiple plausible outcomes. Australia's 54% win probability sits well above the draw rate of 29%, yet the draw remains a meaningful possibility rather than a peripheral one. Egypt's path to victory, at 17%, is narrow but non-negligible; the fixture is not one-sided in structure, even if the model leans clearly toward the favourite.

Model and market are sharply misaligned. The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both favour Egypt substantially, placing the side at roughly 38% to win compared to the model's 17%—a gap of 21 percentage points. The market also rates Australia's win probability at 29%, well below the model's 54%. The dashboard makes no claim as to which assessment is correct; the divergence is substantial enough to flag for users.
